title.zh: 预告奸人当道的四角羊目之马
title.en: The Four-Horned, Goat-Eyed Horse That Heralds Schemers

summary.zh: 䃌山的怪兽：马的身形，羊的眼睛，头顶四角，牛的尾巴，吼声如狗嗥；它一出现，国中就多出奸猾之徒。
summary.en: A beast of Zhu Mountain: a horse's frame with goat's eyes, four horns, and an ox's tail, howling like a dog. When it appears, the state fills with cunning schemers.

description.zh:
东次二经的䃌山南临䃌水，向东可以望见湖泽。山中有兽名峳峳：大体是一匹马，却嵌着羊的眼睛，头顶生出四只角，身后拖一条牛尾，吼叫起来是狗嗥的声音。四种家畜的零件拼成一匹说不清是什么的坐骑模样的兽，游荡在临水的山坡上。

它的兆应在《山海经》灾异谱系里别具一格：“见则其国多狡客。”旱涝瘟疫都不在它名下，它预告人祸：峳峳现身之国，将有大批奸猾之徒横行。灾异应在人心与政局上，这类兆应在全书屈指可数，与朱獳的“国有恐”互为表里：一个预告恐慌的情绪，一个预告作乱的人。历代注家由此多把峳峳读作政治性的凶兆：小人将要得志。

同一座䃌山上还栖着絜钩，一种野鸭模样、长着老鼠尾巴、善于爬树的鸟，见则其国多疫。一山双兆，一主奸人、一主瘟疫，䃌山在东次二经里是不折不扣的凶山。
description.en:
Zhu Mountain, in the second eastern guideway, overlooks the Zhu River to the south and a lake country to the east. Its beast is the youyou: in the main a horse, but set with a goat's eyes, crowned with four horns, and finished with an ox's tail, its cry the howl of a dog. Parts from four barnyard animals assemble into something like a mount that no one would saddle, ranging a hillside above the water.

Its omen is a rarity in the classic's registry of disasters: 'when it is seen, the state abounds in cunning schemers.' It answers for no drought, flood, or pestilence; wherever the youyou appears, the devious will multiply and thrive. Only a handful of portents in the book land on politics and human character rather than weather, and the youyou pairs naturally with the zhuru, herald of panic: one announces the mood of a crisis, the other the men who will exploit it. Commentators accordingly read the beast as a political warning: small men are about to have their day.

The same mountain keeps the jiegou, a duck-shaped, rat-tailed bird that climbs trees, whose appearance brings epidemics. One mountain, two omens — schemers and sickness. Zhu Mountain earns its place among the unluckiest addresses of the eastern guideways.

value.en: Horse's body, goat's eyes, four horns, ox's tail
Its appearance fills the state with schemers

sources.zh: 《山海经·东山经》（东次二经·䃌山）
sources.en: Classic of Mountains and Seas, 'Eastern Mountains' (Zhu Mountain)
